A front-runner could deposit before the user to      change which validators are funded."},"test_reentrancy_on_receive()":{"details":"The receive() function calls _deposit() which makes external calls.      Since deposit()/receive() don't have reentrancy guards, test if      a malicious contract can reenter during deposit."},"test_removeValidators_swap_state()":{"details":"When deleting a non-last validator, the last element is swapped into      the deleted position. returns.excludedArtifacts_: The list of excluded artifact identifiers.

returns.excludedContracts_: The list of excluded contract addresses.

returns.excludedSelectors_: The list of excluded selector configurations.

returns.excludedSenders_: The list of excluded sender addresses.

returns._0: True if any assertion has failed, false otherwise.

returns.targetedArtifactSelectors_: The list of targeted artifact-selector configurations.

returns.targetedArtifacts_: The list of targeted artifact identifiers.

returns.targetedContracts_: The list of targeted contract addresses.

returns.targetedInterfaces_: The list of targeted address-interface configurations.

returns.targetedSelectors_: The list of targeted selector configurations.

returns.targetedSenders_: The list of targeted sender addresses.

test_admin_fee_redirection().details: Admin can change feeRecipient to any address. This is by design      but represents centralization risk.

test_balance_check_selfdestruct_resilien.details: The balance check pattern is:      targetBalance = address(this).balance - DEPOSIT_SIZE      // external call      if (address(this).balance != targetBalance) revert      A self-destruct forcing ETH to this contract between subtraction and check      would cause revert, but not fund loss

test_commission_precision_loss().details: The EL dispatcher computes:      globalFee = (balance * getGlobalFee()) / BASIS_POINTS      operatorFee = (globalFee * getOperatorFee()) / BASIS_POINTS      These truncations can accumulate dust

test_deposit_frontrunning().details: The _deposit() function reads totalAvailableValidators then deposits      sequentially. A front-runner could deposit before the user to      change which validators are funded.

test_reentrancy_on_receive().details: The receive() function calls _deposit() which makes external calls.      Since deposit()/receive() don't have reentrancy guards, test if      a malicious contract can reenter during deposit.

test_removeValidators_swap_state().details: When deleting a non-last validator, the last element is swapped into      the deleted position. This test verifies the state is consistent.

params.balance: The balance being withdrawn (EL or CL rewards)
params.globalFeeBPS: The global fee in basis points
params.operatorFeeBPS: The operator fee in basis points

test_unbounded_gas_addValidators().details: Finding: CALLS-LOOP in addValidators - each key requires SHA256      precompile calls for duplicate detection, creating an unbounded      gas loop. The gas cost is O(n) with ~118k gas per key.

test_unbounded_gas_loop().details: The loop in batchWithdrawELFee iterates over all provided keys.      This test verifies the gas cost scales linearly.

failed().notice: Returns true if any test assertion has failed.

targetSenders().notice: Returns sender allowlist configured via `targetSender` (empty means no sender allowlist).

test_admin_fee_redirection().notice: Tests if admin can redirect operator fees via deactivateOperator

test_balance_check_selfdestruct_resilien.notice: Tests if a self-destruct can break the balance check in _depositValidator

test_commission_precision_loss().notice: Tests for rounding issues in commission calculation

test_deposit_frontrunning().notice: Tests if deposits can be front-run to manipulate allocation

test_duplicate_key_prevention().notice: Tests if the same key can be registered twice

test_initialize_once().notice: Tests that initialize_1 can only be called once

test_max_operator_count().notice: Tests that only 1 operator can be added

test_operator_fee_limit().notice: Tests the operator fee limit enforcement

test_reentrancy_on_receive().notice: Tests if receive() can be exploited via reentrancy

test_removeValidators_funded_protection(.notice: Tests that removeValidators prevents deletion of funded validators

test_removeValidators_swap_state().notice: Tests that removeValidators correctly handles the swap-and-pop         pattern and doesn't orphan state

test_removeValidators_unsorted_indexes().notice: Tests that indexes must be in strictly decreasing order

test_reward_accounting(uint256,uint256,u.notice: Fuzz test that reward calculations don't overflow or have         significant precision loss

test_unauthorized_set_limit().notice: Tests that only admin can set operator limit

test_unauthorized_validator_addition().notice: Tests that non-operators cannot add validators

test_unbounded_gas_addValidators().notice: Tests if addValidators can be called with many keys to cause         gas griefing or DoS. This VULNERABILITY IS CONFIRMED: 10 keys         consume ~1.18M gas (~118k per key), which scales linearly and         could hit block gas limits with ~25+ keys.

test_unbounded_gas_loop().notice: Tests if batchWithdraw can be called with many keys to cause         out-of-gas conditions
